questions.question:
Which one of the following well-formed formulae in predicate calculus is NOT valid?

(GATE CSE 2016 SET-2)
options:
A.

\((\forall x p(x)\Rightarrow \forall xq(x))\Rightarrow (\exists x\neg p(x) \vee \forall xq(x))\)

D.

\(\forall x (p(x)\vee q(x))\Rightarrow (\forall x p(x) \vee \forall x q(x)) \)
questions.correct_answer:
D.

\(\forall x (p(x)\vee q(x))\Rightarrow (\forall x p(x) \vee \forall x q(x)) \)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
